title: AI Governance Requirements
description: Per-control requirements organized in three columns: legal/regulatory, technical, and governance/organizational.

legal: Establish AI governance body with defined authority (EU Art. 9, 17)
Document AI governance roles and responsibilities (ISO 5.3)
Define scope of AI activities within governance framework
technical: Implement governance tooling for AI system inventory
Maintain version-controlled governance documentation
governance: Appoint AI governance lead with board-level reporting (NGAIGE Accountability)
Conduct annual governance framework review
Establish governance committee with cross-functional representation

legal: Conduct risk assessment before AI system deployment (EU Art. 9)
Classify AI systems by risk level (EU Art. 6)
Document risk assessment results and mitigation measures
technical: Implement risk scoring methodology aligned with NIST MAP function
Automate risk monitoring dashboards
Integrate risk assessment into CI/CD pipelines
governance: Define risk appetite and tolerance levels for AI systems (NIST GOVERN-1.3)
Establish risk escalation procedures
Review risk assessments at defined intervals (ISO 6.1)

legal: Ensure training data complies with data protection law (EU Art. 10)
Document data provenance and lawful basis for processing
Respect IP rights in training data (EU Art. 53 for GPAI)
technical: Implement data lineage tracking and cataloging
Automate data quality checks (completeness, accuracy, representativeness)
Deploy bias detection tools on training datasets
governance: Define data governance policies for AI training data (NGAIGE Privacy)
Establish data steward roles for AI datasets
Conduct regular data quality audits (NIST MAP-2.1)

legal: Test for bias across protected characteristics (EU Art. 10)
Document bias testing methodology and results
Implement corrective measures for identified bias
technical: Deploy fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, calibration)
Implement bias detection in model validation pipeline
Use debiasing techniques (resampling, adversarial debiasing, post-processing)
governance: Define acceptable fairness thresholds per use case (NGAIGE Fairness)
Establish diverse review panels for bias assessment
Conduct regular bias audits (NIST MEASURE-2.6, 2.11)

legal: Validate AI system accuracy and robustness (EU Art. 15)
Document validation methodology and benchmark results
Re-validate after significant changes
technical: Implement holdout test sets and cross-validation
Conduct adversarial robustness testing
Benchmark against established performance metrics
governance: Define validation criteria and acceptance thresholds (ISO A.6.2.3)
Establish independent validation team
Maintain validation records for audit (NIST MEASURE-2.5)

legal: Disclose AI system use to affected individuals (EU Art. 50)
Provide right to explanation for automated decisions (EU Art. 85)
Label AI-generated content including deepfakes (EU Art. 50)
technical: Implement explainability methods (SHAP, LIME, attention visualization)
Build user-facing explanation interfaces
Automate AI content labeling and watermarking
governance: Define transparency policies by AI system category (NGAIGE Transparency)
Publish AI system registry or inventory
Train staff on transparency obligations (ISO A.4.3-A.4.5)

legal: Implement human oversight with ability to override AI decisions (EU Art. 14)
Ensure human operators can interrupt AI system operation
Document oversight procedures and escalation paths
technical: Build human-in-the-loop decision workflows
Implement kill switches and override controls
Provide real-time monitoring dashboards for operators
governance: Define human oversight requirements per risk level (NGAIGE Reliability)
Train human operators on AI system limitations
Establish escalation procedures for AI failures (NIST MANAGE-2.4)

legal: Report serious incidents to authorities (EU Art. 72)
Maintain incident records for audit
Notify affected individuals of AI-related incidents
technical: Implement AI-specific incident detection and alerting
Automate incident classification and triage
Maintain rollback capability for model versions
governance: Define AI incident response procedures (NIST MANAGE-4)
Conduct post-incident reviews and root cause analysis
Update risk assessments based on incident learnings (ISO A.9.3)

legal: Maintain quality management system for AI (EU Art. 17)
Support external audit and conformity assessment (EU Art. 43)
Retain audit logs for minimum required period
technical: Implement comprehensive logging for AI decisions (ISO A.9.4)
Automate audit trail generation
Provide audit data export capabilities
governance: Schedule regular internal AI audits (ISO 9.2)
Engage independent third-party auditors
Track audit findings and corrective actions (NGAIGE Accountability)

legal: Conduct fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI (EU Art. 27)
Assess impact on vulnerable groups and protected characteristics
Document mitigation measures for identified impacts
technical: Implement impact measurement frameworks
Deploy stakeholder feedback collection systems
Monitor societal impact metrics post-deployment
governance: Engage affected communities in impact assessment (UNESCO)
Publish impact assessment summaries
Review impacts at defined intervals (NIST MAP-5, OECD Principle 1)
